summary: Rachel Torres (CSM) met with Sandra Keating (customer in insurance/financial services) to discuss Detect product roadmap and feature requests. Sandra raised two primary feature gaps: granular alerting thresholds by entity type with custom behavioral baselines, and in-product pipeline health visibility following a six-hour outage in March. Rachel shared upcoming roadmap items including native LogVault integration and a threat intelligence correlation layer, and the call closed with a discussion of Comply v2's new SOC 2 on-demand reporting feature, with a demo planned for Sandra and her compliance lead Marcus.
actionItems: Rachel Torres: Log formal feature requests for entity grouping with custom behavioral baselines and in-product pipeline health status with proactive degradation alerts
Rachel Torres: Send Sandra confirmed threat intel feed list for correlation layer (including FS-ISAC status) and LogVault native connector timeline details
Rachel Torres: Schedule Comply v2 demo with Sandra and Marcus during the week of April 20th
Rachel Torres: Connect Sandra with product team to provide direct context on entity grouping and custom baseline requirements

keyMoments.text: Sandra describes alert fatigue caused by one-size-fits-all thresholds, noting SOC team is overwhelmed by false positives and unable to focus on real threats
keyMoments.speaker: Sandra Keating

keyMoments.text: Sandra raises the March six-hour outage and its regulatory implications, noting her team only discovered the issue because alert volume dropped to zero
keyMoments.speaker: Sandra Keating

keyMoments.text: Rachel acknowledges the lack of in-product pipeline health visibility as a product gap and commits to logging it as a formal feature request
keyMoments.speaker: Rachel Torres

keyMoments.text: Sandra reacts positively to Comply v2's on-demand SOC 2 reporting, noting it could save significant pain ahead of their upcoming renewal
keyMoments.speaker: Sandra Keating
